 {"id":"bd-3e1t.1.7","title":"API: expose diff strategy config + defaults","description":"Background
- API changes are allowed; strategy selection should be configurable for experimentation.

Task
- Add a `DiffStrategyConfig` (or similar) to runtime config with:
  - enable/disable Bayesian selection
  - cost model constants
  - dirty-row usage toggle
  - reset policy toggles (e.g., reset on log invalidation)
- Define safe defaults that preserve current behavior or improve it conservatively.

Acceptance Criteria
- Config is documented and wired into `TerminalWriter`.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:21.282939455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:55:26.986416556Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T22:55:26.986397109Z","close_reason":"Added RuntimeDiffConfig to ProgramConfig + wired into TerminalWriter","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:21.282939455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:27.649911063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.1.8","title":"Handle diff state reset on log writes/scroll/size changes","description":"Background
- Inline log writes can scroll the terminal and invalidate the UI region unless a scroll region is active.
- TerminalWriter already invalidates prev_buffer in some cases; the new diff strategy must also reset its Bayesian state and dirty-row assumptions when invalidation happens.

Task
- Define and implement a reset policy for diff strategy state when:
  - log writes occur without scroll-region protection
  - terminal size changes or mode toggles invalidate prior buffers
  - full redraw is forced due to dimension mismatch
- Ensure posterior and dirty-row assumptions are reset or re-primed to avoid under-diffing.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic reset logic with explicit conditions.
- Tests cover log-write invalidation + subsequent frame correctness.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:26:15.155006048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:54:40.530809455Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T22:54:40.530789136Z","close_reason":"Reset policy implemented and configurable: (1) Log writes without scroll region protection reset via reset_diff_strategy(). (2) Terminal resize resets via reset_diff_on_resize(). (3) Auto UI height changes reset. (4) Clear screen resets. (5) Dimension mismatches force FullRedraw. (6) RuntimeDiffConfig.reset_on_invalidation and reset_on_resize toggles allow disabling resets when not needed. Tests cover log-write invalidation scenarios in terminal_writer::tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:26:15.155006048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:26:27.312337843Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.1.9","title":"Calibrate diff cost constants from microbench data","description":"Background
- Expected-cost decisions rely on constants (scan/emit/overhead). Defaults should be grounded in data, especially for large screens.

Task
- Use the microbench data (A6) to set or validate default cost constants.
- Document the calibration method and the resulting defaults in config docs.

Acceptance Criteria
- Defaults are backed by measured data and preserve conservative correctness.
- Changes are documented and reproducible.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:26:21.309056803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T23:06:32.201879883Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T23:06:32.201862380Z","close_reason":"Calibrated diff cost defaults from perf_diff_microbench; documented repro","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:26:21.309056803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:26:27.720636492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:26:27.917205667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.10","title":"Smith's rule scheduling for effect queue (min weighted completion time)","description":"Background\n- Effects/commands vary in cost and importance; FIFO can inflate tail latency.\n\nTask\n- Use Smith's rule (order by p_i / w_i) with estimated durations and weights.\n- Provide a small simulator/benchmark to compare against FIFO.\n- Integrate into runtime queue when enabled.\n\nAcceptance\n- Simulation shows lower mean + tail completion time vs FIFO on mixed workloads.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:44:02.709590739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T02:44:02.709590739Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.10","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:44:02.709590739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.2","title":"BOCPD Resize Coalescer Upgrade","description":"Background
- Resize coalescer currently uses a heuristic burst/steady regime with hysteresis thresholds.
- We want a mathematically principled, adaptive regime detector with formal properties.

Goal
- Replace heuristic regime detection with Bayesian Online Change-Point Detection (BOCPD) to classify steady vs burst behavior.
- Preserve existing invariants: latest-wins, bounded latency, deterministic decisions.

Key Concepts
- Run-length posterior over regime changes, hazard function for change probability.
- Decision uses posterior mass over burst regime to choose coalescing delay.
- Computation kept O(K) with run-length truncation.

Success Criteria
- Coalescer remains deterministic and respects hard deadlines.
- Fewer wasted renders during resize storms with no additional latency in steady mode.
- Decision logs include posterior summary for auditability.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:14.516666595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T23:00:53.918070141Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T23:00:53.918050614Z","close_reason":"BOCPD integration complete. Core functionality: (1) BocpdDetector in bocpd.rs with exponential hazard + run-length truncation at K=100. (2) Integrated into ResizeCoalescer via enable_bocpd config. (3) update_regime() uses BOCPD with heuristic fallback. (4) Accessor methods: bocpd_p_burst(), bocpd_recommended_delay(). (5) 13 BOCPD tests + 4 property tests pass. Remaining P3 subtasks (2.4 logging, 2.6 simulation) are optional enhancements.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:14.516666595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.2.1","title":"BOCPD design: hazard, run-length cap, decision rule","description":"Background
- We need a formal regime detector for resize bursts that replaces heuristic thresholds.

Task
- Define BOCPD model: observation = inter-arrival time or event rate; hazard function H(r);
  predictive likelihood for steady vs burst regimes; run-length truncation K for O(K) update.
- Specify decision rule: use posterior mass on burst regime to select coalescing delay.

Details
- Choose observation model (e.g., exponential for inter-arrival, or Poisson for counts).
- Hazard: constant hazard or adaptive based on recent rate.
- Provide defaults tuned for responsiveness on large terminals.

Acceptance Criteria
- Design doc in issue description is implementable as-is.
- Explicit complexity bounds and deterministic update rules.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:24.375476337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:33:05.533775751Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T22:33:05.533751866Z","close_reason":"Implemented BOCPD design with hazard function, run-length cap (K=100), exponential observation model for inter-arrival times, and decision rule using P(burst) thresholds. Includes 13 unit tests covering initial state, burst/steady detection, regime transitions, determinism, posterior normalization, and property tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:24.375476337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.2.2","title":"Implement BOCPD state + update logic in resize coalescer","description":"Background
- BOCPD requires maintaining a run-length posterior and updating each resize event.

Task
- Implement BOCPD update step inside `resize_coalescer.rs` (or a small internal struct).
- Keep memory bounded with run-length truncation.

Constraints
- Deterministic updates, no floating-point nondeterminism beyond standard f64.
- No allocations in hot path beyond bounded buffers.

Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests confirm posterior sums to ~1 (within epsilon) and update is stable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:29.847358657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:38:22.557903198Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T22:38:22.557885475Z","close_reason":"Implemented BOCPD state and update logic in resize coalescer. Added enable_bocpd config option, optional BocpdDetector in ResizeCoalescer, integrated BOCPD into update_regime with heuristic fallback. Added 9 unit tests for posterior normalization, burst/steady detection, determinism, memory bounds, and mixed traffic stability.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:29.847358657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:54.823321194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3e1t.4.8","title":"E2E: extend scripts/e2e_test.sh for large-screen runs + evidence logs","description":"Background
- End-to-end coverage should exercise inline + alt-screen on large terminals.

Task
- Extend `scripts/e2e_test.sh` to run large-screen scenarios (200x60, 240x80) for both inline and alt-screen.
- Capture evidence JSONL logs and include them in artifacts.
- Add clear, structured logging in the script (timestamps, config echo, failures).
- Support deterministic runs with explicit seed and log the seed.
- Log terminal capabilities and key env vars used in the run.

Acceptance Criteria
- Script is non-interactive and CI-friendly.
- Logs include scenario name, screen mode, size, seed, capabilities, and evidence log path.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:21:52.694152681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T02:33:23.701154397Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.4.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:21:52.694152681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.4.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:22:15.869931737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.4.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:22:16.075972856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.4.9","title":"E2E: extend scripts/widget_api_e2e.sh with policy toggles + evidence logs","description":"Background
- Widget API coverage should include new diff/resize/budget policy toggles.

Task
- Extend `scripts/widget_api_e2e.sh` to run scenarios with:
  - Bayesian diff strategy enabled/disabled
  - BOCPD coalescer enabled/disabled
  - Conformal budget gate enabled/disabled
- Capture evidence JSONL logs for each configuration and emit structured script logs.
- Support deterministic runs with explicit seed and log the seed.
- Log terminal capabilities and key env vars used in the run.

Acceptance Criteria
- Script remains deterministic and CI-friendly.
- Each configuration has a distinct log directory with metadata.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:21:58.152755985Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T02:12:41.653322809Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T02:12:41.653304825Z","close_reason":"Added harness env toggles (diff/BOCPD/conformal) + evidence JSONL and extended widget_api_e2e.sh with policy matrix + per-case logs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.4.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:22:16.482167168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.4.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:22:16.688856109Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.4.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:22:16.895151407Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.4.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:21:58.152755985Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.4.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:22:16.279527680Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5","title":"Optimize demo visual effects for instant load + 60fps","description":"Profile demo showcase visual effects screen; identify hotspots; optimize to reduce frame time and load time while keeping determinism. Apply extreme optimization workflow with baseline, flamegraph, opportunity matrix, and isomorphism proof. Add tests or benchmarks if needed.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T01:59:30.622888478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T01:59:30.622888478Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T01:59:30.622888478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.6","title":"Dirty-span tracking for sparse diff (interval union)","description":"Background\n- Dirty rows are coarse; sparse edits still scan full rows.\n\nTask\n- Track per-row dirty spans (interval union) on writes.\n- Use spans to scan only affected ranges in diff (plus optional guard bands).\n- Keep spans merged and bounded in O(k) where k is span count.\n\nAcceptance\n- diff benches show a measurable win for 1-row + 5% sparse cases.\n- Diff results are bit-identical to full compute.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:43:40.638995632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T02:44:10.072366552Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:43:40.638995632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.7","title":"Blockwise diff via summed-area table (skip empty tiles)","description":"Background\n- Large screens + tiny changes still trigger full row scans.\n\nTask\n- Build a per-frame change bitmap and 2D prefix-sum (summed-area table).\n- Use a tile/quadtree walk to skip blocks with zero change.\n- Fall back to row-scan inside dirty tiles only.\n\nAcceptance\n- large_screen 2% benchmarks show measurable improvement without regressions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:43:46.806303425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T02:43:46.806303425Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:43:46.806303425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.8","title":"Bayesian diff-strategy selector (Beta-Binomial expected cost)","description":"Background\n- compute vs compute_dirty efficiency depends on sparsity; static choice can be suboptimal.\n\nTask\n- Use a Beta-Binomial estimator for change density or dirty-span density.\n- Compute expected cost for full vs dirty vs span scan and pick the minimum.\n- Emit a small evidence log entry with posterior mean and chosen path.\n\nAcceptance\n- Selector never worse than the best fixed strategy by more than 5% on diff_bench scenarios.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:43:51.769186850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T02:43:51.769186850Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:43:51.769186850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.9","title":"Submodular widget refresh selection under frame budget","description":"Background\n- When over budget, we need a principled way to choose which optional widgets update.\n\nTask\n- Model widget value as a monotone submodular function.\n- Use greedy selection within the time budget (1-1/e guarantee).\n- Integrate with degradation hooks (is_essential).\n\nAcceptance\n- Under forced budget, greedy policy outperforms FIFO/round-robin in a synthetic demo.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:43:57.680369644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T02:43:57.680369644Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:43:57.680369644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e8","title":"Implement structured logging with tracing","description":"INFRASTRUCTURE - LOGGING & TRACING

## Background
Comprehensive logging is critical for debugging terminal rendering issues, performance analysis, and understanding execution flow. Must support both development debugging and production diagnostics.

## Technical Requirements

### Tracing Infrastructure
- Use tracing crate for structured, async-aware logging
- Log levels: ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE
- Span-based instrumentation for render pipeline
- Subscriber configuration for:
  - Development: pretty-printed to stderr
  - Testing: captured for assertions
  - Production: JSON structured output (optional)

### Key Instrumentation Points
- Frame lifecycle: start → render → diff → present → complete
- Widget rendering: widget type, area, render time
- Buffer operations: resize, clear, set (at TRACE level)
- Input parsing: raw bytes → parsed events
- State changes: model updates, command execution
- Error conditions: with full context

### Log Format
Development:
  2026-01-31T12:00:00.123Z INFO ftui::render frame_start frame=42 widgets=15
  2026-01-31T12:00:00.125Z DEBUG ftui::diff changed_cells=120 skip_rows=10
  
Testing (structured capture):
  LogEntry { level: INFO, target: "ftui::render", msg: "frame_start", ... }

### Feature Gates
- feature = "tracing" - enables all instrumentation
- feature = "tracing-json" - JSON subscriber for production
- Default: tracing disabled for minimal overhead

## Design Rationale
Terminal UIs are notoriously hard to debug. Structured tracing with spans enables understanding complex render pipelines. Feature-gated to avoid overhead in release builds.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tracing subscriber configuration
- [ ] Frame-level span instrumentation
- [ ] Widget render spans with timing
- [ ] Input parsing tracing
- [ ] Error context capture
- [ ] Test log capture mechanism
- [ ] Feature gate documentation
- [ ] Example showing log output

## Performance Budget
- Disabled: zero overhead (compiled out)
- Enabled: < 1µs per span enter/exit

## Location: ftui-core crate (feature = "tracing")","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:27:56.000241382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T17:54:34.427170543Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T17:54:34.427143291Z","close_reason":"Implemented structured logging with tracing crate. Created logging.rs with init_dev(), init_json(), no-op macros when disabled. Added tracing/tracing-json feature flags. All 71 tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e8","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3emq","title":"Async Validation: Deterministic Concurrency Contract + Golden Trace","description":"## Goal
Make async validation behavior deterministic under a seeded scheduler and prove no stale results can override newer input.

## Core Idea (Extreme Optimization + Correctness)
- Monotonic validation token per input change; results must include token.
- Apply result only if token matches current input token.
- Record validation events in a trace (start/cancel/complete/apply).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Stale results are always ignored with explicit log entries.
- Deterministic trace under fixed seed and scripted input.
- Golden trace checksum matches after refactors (isomorphism proof).

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_token_monotonic: tokens strictly increase per input.
- unit_stale_result_ignored: older token never applies.
- unit_trace_contains_all_events: no missing transitions.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Seeded async validator test harness with golden trace hash.
- Log: token, state, duration, decision per validation.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:09:10.342042338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:48:47.828656369Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:48:47.828634127Z","close_reason":"Completed: Implemented async validation with deterministic concurrency - monotonic tokens, staleness prevention, event tracing with golden checksums, 17 unit tests passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3fu8","title":"Semantic Events: Core Types and SemanticEvent Enum","description":"## Task

Define the core `SemanticEvent` enum and related types in `ftui-core`.

## Implementation

Create `crates/ftui-core/src/semantic_event.rs`:

```rust
use crate::{KeyCode, Modifiers, MouseButton, Position};
use std::time::Duration;

/// High-level semantic events derived from raw terminal input.
/// These represent user intentions rather than raw input.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
pub enum SemanticEvent {
    // === Mouse Gestures ===
    
    /// Single click (mouse down + up in same position)
    Click {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Two clicks within threshold time
    DoubleClick {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Three clicks within threshold (often used for line selection)
    TripleClick {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Mouse held down beyond threshold without moving
    LongPress {
        pos: Position,
        duration: Duration,
    },
    
    // === Drag Gestures ===
    
    /// Mouse moved beyond drag threshold while button held
    DragStart {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Ongoing drag movement
    DragMove {
        start: Position,
        current: Position,
        delta: (i16, i16),  // Movement since last DragMove
    },
    
    /// Mouse released after drag
    DragEnd {
        start: Position,
        end: Position,
    },
    
    /// Drag cancelled (Escape pressed, focus lost, etc.)
    DragCancel,
    
    // === Keyboard Gestures ===
    
    /// Key chord sequence completed (e.g., Ctrl+K Ctrl+C)
    Chord {
        sequence: Vec<ChordKey>,
    },
    
    // === Touch-Like Gestures ===
    
    /// Swipe gesture (rapid mouse movement)
    Swipe {
        direction: SwipeDirection,
        distance: u16,
        velocity: f32,  // cells per second
    },
}

/// A key in a chord sequence
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct ChordKey {
    pub code: KeyCode,
    pub modifiers: Modifiers,
}

/// Direction for swipe gestures
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum SwipeDirection {
    Up,
    Down,
    Left,
    Right,
}

impl SemanticEvent {
    /// Returns true if this is a drag-related event
    pub fn is_drag(&self) -> bool {
        matches\!(self, Self::DragStart { .. } | Self::DragMove { .. } | Self::DragEnd { .. } | Self::DragCancel)
    }
    
    /// Returns the position if this event has one
    pub fn position(&self) -> Option<Position> {
        match self {
            Self::Click { pos, .. } |
            Self::DoubleClick { pos, .. } |
            Self::TripleClick { pos, .. } |
            Self::LongPress { pos, .. } |
            Self::DragStart { pos, .. } => Some(*pos),
            Self::DragMove { current, .. } => Some(*current),
            Self::DragEnd { end, .. } => Some(*end),
            _ => None,
        }
    }
}
```

## Unit Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn semantic_event_is_drag() {
        assert\!(SemanticEvent::DragStart { 
            pos: Position { x: 0, y: 0 }, 
            button: MouseButton::Left 
        }.is_drag());
        
        assert\!(\!SemanticEvent::Click { 
            pos: Position { x: 0, y: 0 }, 
            button: MouseButton::Left 
        }.is_drag());
    }

    #[test]
    fn semantic_event_position() {
        let click = SemanticEvent::Click { 
            pos: Position { x: 5, y: 10 }, 
            button: MouseButton::Left 
        };
        assert_eq\!(click.position(), Some(Position { x: 5, y: 10 }));
        
        let chord = SemanticEvent::Chord { sequence: vec\![] };
        assert_eq\!(chord.position(), None);
    }

    #[test]
    fn chord_key_equality() {
        let k1 = ChordKey { code: KeyCode::Char('k'), modifiers: Modifiers::CONTROL };
        let k2 = ChordKey { code: KeyCode::Char('k'), modifiers: Modifiers::CONTROL };
        let k3 = ChordKey { code: KeyCode::Char('c'), modifiers: Modifiers::CONTROL };
        
        assert_eq\!(k1, k2);
        assert_ne\!(k1, k3);
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SemanticEvent enum with all variants
- [ ] ChordKey and SwipeDirection types
- [ ] Helper methods (is_drag, position)
- [ ] Unit tests passing
- [ ] Exported from ftui-core lib.rs
- [ ] Documentation with examples

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.
